content.thinking: The user ran `git diff > /tmp/a.diff` but it failed because `/tmp` is not a git repository. The file `/tmp/a.diff` is likely empty. Then they ran `cat /tmp/a.diff`. Let me read the file to see what's in it.

file.content:
diff --git i/scripts/clog.sh w/scripts/clog.sh
index 5c5ff12..ae09c96 100755
--- i/scripts/clog.sh
+++ w/scripts/clog.sh
@@ -1,6 +1,10 @@
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
-# Show recent commits from both claude repos
 
-claude-dotfiles log --oneline --stat -20 >/tmp/clog.txt
-echo "::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::" >>/tmp/clog.txt
-git log --oneline --stat -20 >>/tmp/clog.txt
+BARE=/mnt/sda1/claude-global.git
+
+echo "=== bare repo ==="
+git --git-dir="$BARE" --work-tree="$HOME" log --oneline -20
+
+echo ""
+echo "=== local repo ==="
+git -C "$HOME/claude" log --oneline -20
diff --git i/scripts/csync.sh w/scripts/csync.sh
index d2f2420..0fd63ad 100755
--- i/scripts/csync.sh
+++ w/scripts/csync.sh
@@ -1,20 +1,25 @@
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
-# Auto-commit changes in both claude repos
+set -euo pipefail
 
-TS="$(date +%H:%M:%S)"
+LOCK="/tmp/csync.lock"
+exec 9>"$LOCK"
+flock -n 9 || exit 0
 
-# sync important dirs to ./global/ (add-only, never delete)
-for dir in cache plans memories teams tasks projects transcripts session-env usage-data commands agents skills hooks; do
-  [ -d ~/.claude/$dir ] && rsync -a ~/.claude/$dir/ ./global/$dir/
+TS=$(date +%H:%M:%S)
+BARE=/mnt/sda1/claude-global.git
+
+# rsync ~/.claude/ state into ~/claude/global/
+for d in cache plans memories teams tasks projects transcripts session-env usage-data commands agents skills hooks; do
+  [ -d "$HOME/.claude/$d" ] && rsync -a "$HOME/.claude/$d/" "$HOME/claude/global/$d/"
 done
 
-# bare repo: everything in ~/.claude/
-./scripts/cgit.sh add ~/.claude/
-./scripts/cgit.sh add -u
-./scripts/cgit.sh commit -m "auto-sync $TS" --no-verify || true
+# bare repo — tracks ~/.claude/
+git --git-dir="$BARE" --work-tree="$HOME" add "$HOME/.claude/"
+git --git-dir="$BARE" --work-tree="$HOME" add -u
+git --git-dir="$BARE" --work-tree="$HOME" commit -m "auto-sync $TS" --no-verify || true
+git --git-dir="$BARE" --work-tree="$HOME" push || true
 
-# home repo
-git add -A
-git commit -m "auto-sync $TS" --no-verify || true
-git push
-claude-dotfiles push
+# local repo — tracks ~/claude/
+git -C "$HOME/claude" add -A
+git -C "$HOME/claude" commit -m "auto-sync $TS" --no-verify || true
+git -C "$HOME/claude" push || true

content.text:
The diff shows changes to two scripts in what looks like a Claude dotfiles sync setup:

**`scripts/clog.sh`** — Rewritten to show logs from two repos:
- A bare repo at `/mnt/sda1/claude-global.git` (tracking `~/.claude/`)
- A local repo at `~/claude/`
- Replaces the old `claude-dotfiles` alias approach with explicit `--git-dir`/`--work-tree` flags

**`scripts/csync.sh`** — Rewritten auto-commit/sync script:
- Adds `set -euo pipefail` and flock-based locking (`/tmp/csync.lock`) to prevent concurrent runs
- Switches from `./scripts/cgit.sh` wrapper to direct `git --git-dir="$BARE" --work-tree="$HOME"` commands
- Switches from relative paths to explicit `$HOME/claude/` and `$HOME/.claude/` paths
- Adds `|| true` to push commands so failures don't abort the script
- Drops the `claude-dotfiles` alias entirely

Looks like a solid cleanup — makes the two-repo setup (bare repo for `~/.claude/`, regular repo for `~/claude/`) more explicit and robust. The flock guard is a nice addition to prevent race conditions from overlapping cron/timer runs.

Want me to do anything with this diff?
